purpose: Standalone evals exercising the per-finding workflow (Phases 0-5 + Phase 6) on representative cascade scenarios. Baseline runs the prompts without the skill loaded; with-skill runs them with the skill loaded; the per-eval assertions encode what a correct cascade-guarded fix plan must contain. Integration coverage of the codex-review-cycle <-> guard contract is enforced by three mechanisms (no executable test runner): (A) mechanical preflight checks in codex-review-cycle SKILL.md step 8 (Phase 6 note + <batch_reconciliation> + cascade-guard receipts with editable gate_status); (B) manual dogfood scenarios D1-D6 in skills/review-fix-cascade-guard/references/dogfood-checklist.md; (C) the documentation cross-reference invariant documented in SKILL.md References.
common_assertions: Output contains an `Invariant:` line whose body is path-neutral (no specific function/file/line names in the invariant phrasing itself).
Output contains a `Sibling-path matrix:` block listing at least two sibling paths beyond the one codex named.

Output names at least one cascade `Archetype:` from the 7-value enum (`path-coverage`, `state-persistence`, `boundary-binding`, `identity-contract`, `doc-cascade`, `interaction-modality`, `silent-violation`).
Output emits a `gate_status:` line whose value is one of: `closed`, `accepted-residual`, `invariant-unknown`, `high-cascade-risk`, `needs-user-decision`.
Output contains a `Likely next-cycle finding:` line predicting a plausible follow-on attack.
When the fix envelope excludes a valid sibling path, the output marks it explicitly under `Out of scope:` with a one-line reason.

evals.name: Inventory removal coverage
evals.prompt: codex found that breaking a Minecraft machine block deletes its inventory contents instead of dropping them. The cited line is `playerWillDestroy`. Plan the fix.
expectations: Invariant phrased as `<inventory> must be dropped exactly once on every block removal path.` (or equivalent path-neutral wording).
Sibling-path matrix lists at least: explosion, `/setblock`, replacement by piston/minecart/lava, neighbor-update removal, world unload.
Fix envelope does NOT stop at `playerWillDestroy`; it covers `onRemove` / `onBlockBroken` / `onExplosion` (or the framework-equivalent removal hook).
Includes a double-drop guard (avoid dropping when the block was already removed by the same broader operation).

evals.name: Tree tap provenance
evals.prompt: codex says player-placed logs can be tapped infinitely with the new tap gate. Fix the gate.
expectations: Invariant phrased around `tapping is allowed only on naturally-grown logs whose leaves are persistent`, not around `nearby leaf type`.
Matrix considers: player-placed logs, persistent vs decayable leaves, partial decay state, log adjacency to schematic-built trees.
Fix uses provenance/persistent-leaf state, NOT raw block-type proximity.

evals.name: Confirm-host TOCTOU
evals.prompt: codex found that the confirm-host check happens before URL interpolation, so the actual send can hit a different host than the user authorized. Fix it.
expectations: Invariant phrased around `the host authorized by the user must be the exact host used by the backend request`.
Fix binds the resolved URL to the executed request OR moves enforcement into the authoritative request-issuance layer.
Addresses nondeterministic interpolation (random functions, time-based variables, async-resolved variables).
Validation includes a static-URL test AND an interpolated-URL test.

evals.name: Scope-guard ledger schema
evals.prompt: codex says raw findings may leak via the rejected ledger; redesign the ledger to store redacted titles. Plan the fix.
expectations: Matrix names every ledger consumer: scope-guard's own writes, codex-review-cycle's `<rejected>` projection, summary render, audit log.
Fix accounts for field-name compatibility (old vs new shape) and migration of any persisted ledger.
Validation asserts no raw title appears in any caller projection.

evals.name: Plan split compatibility
evals.prompt: codex says extracting a SKILL.md section into a reference file may break existing anchors. Plan the fix.
expectations: Matrix names: anchor stubs in the source SKILL.md, downstream cross-references (other SKILL.md, README, CHANGELOG, sample files), verification grep rules.
Fix preserves anchor resolvability (stub headings or link rewrites) AND keeps verification grep consistent with the new file shape.

evals.name: Audit mutation semantics
evals.prompt: codex says audit logs fire even when nothing changed. Fix it.
expectations: Matrix enumerates: semantic no-op (same value written), bulk import, delete + immediate re-create, async flush durability, error-path partial mutations.
Fix uses a `mutation-happened` flag derived from the actual data delta, not from the call entry point.
Validation includes a no-op write test that asserts no audit row is produced.

evals.name: False-green test runner
evals.prompt: codex says the new smoke-test script can pass even when the intended test is absent or ignored. Fix it.
expectations: Matrix names: zero matched tests, `#[ignore]` / `it.skip` tests, substring vs exact name match, executed-count parsing, missing-field assertions that pass against null/default.
Fix asserts an executed-count > 0 AND an exact test-identity match.
Validation includes a negative-control case where removing/renaming/ignoring the target test makes the gate fail.

evals.name: Canonical artifact identity
evals.prompt: codex says alias lookup works on cold path but fails on warm cache and symlinked jar paths. Fix it.
expectations: Output names ONE authoritative normalized identity (e.g. resolved canonical SHA / canonicalized absolute path).
Matrix names at least two alternate representations the fix reconciles: alias, raw-input path, cache key, display label, namespace descriptor.
Fix routes cold path, warm path, alias, cache key, and lookup through the same canonical identity.

evals.name: Editor input modality
evals.prompt: codex says the variable completion fix works for one cursor but fails for escaped placeholders and multiple cursors. Fix it.
expectations: Matrix names: backend escape syntax parity (`\\{{` etc.), multi-cursor replacement (CodeMirror multiple selections), existing suffix preservation, language fallback when language is unknown.
Fix mirrors backend escape semantics on the frontend completion code path.
Fix applies the completion to every selection range, not just the main cursor.

evals.name: Silent formatter corruption
evals.prompt: codex says XML/HTML auto-format still corrupts whitespace-sensitive payloads on save. Fix it.
expectations: Output prefers fail-closed on save-time silent transformation unless losslessness is proven for every supported markup class.
Matrix lists: `<pre>` reflow, inter-element whitespace, CDATA, preserve-space markers, inline-element neighbors, malformed input.
Fix either proves the transformation is lossless OR moves formatting to a manual/undoable action and removes silent save-time formatting.

evals.name: Traversal mutation
evals.prompt: codex says a tree-traversal plan captures child indexes before splicing siblings. Fix the plan.
expectations: Matrix names: same-parent multi-match, adjacent-sibling cases, reverse-order apply, collect-then-apply, object key ordering.
Fix switches to collect-then-apply OR mutates in reverse index order so captured indexes stay valid.
Includes a regression case with multiple matches in the same parent.

scoring.notes: Pass = (common_assertion_pass_rate * 0.6) + (per_eval_expectation_pass_rate * 0.4) >= 0.75. With-skill runs are expected to pass; baseline runs are expected to fail at least the matrix and gate_status assertions on most prompts. The pre-implementation baseline-vs-with-skill comparison must show at least three historical cascade cases (E01, E03, E07 are the canonical three) prevented or explicitly residualized by the with-skill run.
